subject: Illegal dumping behind 3308 Hirsch Ave
body_clean: Mayor Okonkwo, I live on Hirsch and for the third time this year someone has dumped a pile of old furniture and what looks like construction debris in the alley behind 3308. We're talking a sectional couch, drywall chunks, and bags of concrete. It's been sitting there almost two weeks. I've spoken to my alderman but nothing's moved. Can code enforcement or public works do something before the rats settle in?

subject: Illegal dumping behind 3308 Hirsch Ave
body_clean: Ms. Merritt, thank you for bringing this to our attention. I inspected the alley behind 3308 Hirsch this afternoon. The debris constitutes an illegal dump under Village Code §8-3-4. I've issued a notice of violation and coordinated with Public Works for removal within 72 hours. If the material is not claimed, the Village will abate and bill the adjacent property owner. I'll follow up with you directly once the alley is cleared.

subject: Property on 22nd Ave is becoming a junkyard
body_clean: Hello Mayor, the house at 1904 22nd Ave has gotten completely out of hand. The front and side yards are packed with broken appliances, old tires, and scrap metal piled almost as high as the fence. It's been building since last summer. I understand the owner may be elderly but it's a real eyesore and a safety hazard, and frankly property values on the block are suffering. Please have someone take a look.

subject: Property on 22nd Ave is becoming a junkyard
body_clean: Mr. Telford, I conducted an inspection at 1904 22nd Ave on April 10th. The accumulation of inoperable equipment and debris does violate §8-4-11 (exterior property maintenance) and §10-2-6 (storage of inoperable vehicles and scrap). I've posted a Notice of Violation with a 30-day compliance window. If the property is not brought into compliance by May 10th, the Village will schedule abatement and pursue cost recovery. We are aware of the owner's situation and will handle this with appropriate sensitivity.

subject: Property on 22nd Ave is becoming a junkyard
body_clean: Ms. Pulaski, thank you for the update. I walked by again today and it does look like some things have been removed from the side yard. Still a lot out front but there's progress. I appreciate the Village taking this seriously. Please keep me in the loop if abatement ends up being necessary.

subject: Three cars parked on lawn at 2715 Washington Blvd
body_clean: Mayor Okonkwo, I'm writing about the property at 2715 Washington Blvd. The residents there have had three vehicles — two trucks and a sedan — parked directly on the grass for months. The lawn is now completely torn up and muddy, and every time it rains the runoff goes right into the sidewalk. I've seen code officers drive by but nothing has happened. Is there a specific ordinance that covers this? I'd like to see it enforced.

subject: Three cars parked on lawn at 2715 Washington Blvd
body_clean: Ms. Breckenridge, parking vehicles on a lawn or unpaved surface is prohibited under Village Code §7-1-9(b). I visited 2715 Washington on July 14th and confirmed three vehicles are parked on the grass. A Notice of Violation has been issued requiring the vehicles to be relocated to a paved surface or removed within 14 days. Continued violation will result in administrative fines beginning at $150 per day. Thank you for the report.

subject: Barking dog at 408 Bohland Ave — this has gone on long enough
body_clean: Good morning Mayor. I have been patient but I'm at the end of my rope. The property at 408 Bohland Ave has a large dog that barks nonstop from roughly 6 a.m. until well past 10 p.m. every single day. I work from home and some days I cannot hear my own phone calls. I've knocked on the door twice and the owner was dismissive. I filed a complaint with the police non-emergency line in January but I haven't seen any follow-up. What else can I do?

subject: Barking dog at 408 Bohland Ave — this has gone on long enough
body_clean: Mr. Pham, thank you for reaching out, and I'm sorry this has dragged on. I've forwarded your message to Officer Banks in our community liaison office and asked him to pull the January complaint and follow up personally. Persistent nuisance animal situations like this can be addressed under both our noise ordinance and the animal control code, so there are real tools available. Someone from the department will be in touch with you by end of week.

subject: Short-term rental on St. Paul Ave — parking and noise problems
body_clean: Mayor Okonkwo, a house on our block at 3117 St. Paul Ave has been listed on a vacation rental site for the past several months. Every weekend there's a different group of guests, sometimes 10 or 12 people, with cars parked up and down the street and music until 1 or 2 in the morning. Long-term neighbors feel like we have a hotel next door with no oversight. Does Bellwood have rules for this type of rental? We'd like to know what recourse we have.

subject: Short-term rental on St. Paul Ave — parking and noise problems
body_clean: Ms. Ormond, thank you for bringing this to our attention. The Mayor has asked me to follow up. The Village is in the process of reviewing its short-term rental regulations, and your concern will be forwarded to Code Enforcement and the Village Attorney's office for review of the current ordinance applicability. In the meantime, for late-night noise violations please call the non-emergency line at 708-555-0141 so officers can document and respond in real time. We will be in touch with you once the legal review is complete.

subject: Mattresses dumped on 50th Ave near Granville
body_clean: Hi, this is Darnell Hutchins at 4922 Granville Ave. Walking my dog this morning I found four mattresses and a box spring dumped on the parkway at 50th and Granville. They look like they've been there a few days at least. Concerned about bedbugs spreading to nearby properties. Can the Village get these picked up quickly? I can send photos if that helps.

subject: Mattresses dumped on 50th Ave near Granville
body_clean: Mr. Hutchins, I got your message. We'll have a crew out to 50th and Granville tomorrow morning to pull those mattresses. Bedbugs are a real concern with abandoned bedding — we treat this as priority. If you can send photos to the public works email that would help us document for the illegal dumping case. We'll also check the alley cameras in the area if there are any. Thank you for calling it in.

subject: Car up on blocks in front yard — 2109 Bellwood Ave
body_clean: Mayor, I noticed the property at 2109 Bellwood Ave has a car sitting on cinder blocks in the front yard. Plates look expired or missing. It's been like that since at least February. I know this is probably a code violation and I wanted to make sure it was on someone's radar. Happy to point out the exact spot on a map if needed.

subject: Tires and engine parts dumped in alley off Madison St
body_clean: Hello Mayor Okonkwo, I'm a resident near Madison Street and I've found a pile of used tires — probably eight or ten of them — along with some auto parts and oil-stained containers dumped in the alley between Madison and Monroe around 19th Ave. I'm worried about standing water in the tires and mosquitoes this summer. Can the Village arrange cleanup? I don't know if there's a way to find out who did this but it's becoming a regular spot.

subject: Vehicles permanently parked on lawn across the street from me
body_clean: Mayor, the family across from me at 3814 Harvard Ave has been parking four vehicles on their front lawn year round for as long as I can remember. I get that parking is tight on this block but the grass is completely gone, it's all ruts and mud now, and in the winter the salt and slush makes a mess that runs into the storm drain. Is there any process to get them to put down gravel or pavers? Or do they just have to move the cars?

subject: Airbnb guests at 2028 25th Ave — noise and garbage
body_clean: Dear Mayor Okonkwo, I live next door to 2028 25th Ave and since April the owner has been renting it out constantly on Airbnb. I've counted at least fourteen different groups. Most are fine honestly, but three weekends in a row we've had people in the backyard until 2 a.m., and last weekend they left two trash bags sitting outside on the parkway for four days. I've tried talking to the owner by text but I get no response. I'd like to know what the Village can actually do.

subject: Barking dog waking my infant — 1208 Monroe St
body_clean: Mayor Okonkwo, I have a four-month-old who has trouble sleeping as it is, and the dog at 1208 Monroe St starts barking between 5:30 and 6 every morning without fail. Some mornings it goes on for 45 minutes. The neighbors have been polite when I've raised it but nothing has actually changed. I'm not trying to get anyone in trouble — I just desperately need some sleep. Is there anything the Village can do to help mediate?

subject: Dump truck unloaded fill dirt in vacant lot on Rice Ave
body_clean: Mayor, I watched from my window last Tuesday around 7 p.m. as a dump truck backed into the vacant lot at the corner of Rice and Geneva and unloaded what looked like several tons of mixed fill dirt and construction debris. No permit signage, no one supervising, and the truck was gone in under ten minutes. The lot sits lower than the road and I'm worried about drainage impacts when it rains. Someone needs to look at this.

subject: Too many cars parked on the grass at the corner house
body_clean: Hello, I'm on the 500 block of Bellwood Ave and the corner property at 502 Bellwood has I think six cars, some with no plates, parked on literally every square foot of the front and side yards. There is no driveway left visible. It makes it really hard to see around the corner when pulling out and I almost had an accident last week. This is a safety issue as much as an eyesore. Please send someone out.

subject: Illegal dump site forming in alley near 25th Ave and Madison
body_clean: Mayor Okonkwo — the alley running behind the 2400 block of Madison has had a steady accumulation of dumped trash, broken TVs, and what looks like restaurant equipment over the past month. I've seen it grow week by week. I walk that alley every morning with my dog and it's getting worse not better. I don't know if it's one person or multiple people but the volume suggests someone is doing commercial dumping. Please have someone investigate.

subject: Short-term rental neighbors never told us — any notice required?
body_clean: Hello Mayor, the property next door to me at 3740 Mannheim Rd-adjacent on Harvard Ave started showing up on rental apps last fall. We found out from a stranger who rang our doorbell thinking our house was the rental. I'm not opposed to it in principle but there are real privacy issues — strangers walking around our shared fence, shining phone lights into the yard at night. Is there any requirement in Bellwood that an owner notify adjacent neighbors before starting a short-term rental? This seems like it should be a thing.
